label: Product Description
description: Write conversion-focused product copy with benefits and SEO

fields.label: Product name
fields.placeholder: TrailLite 45L Hiking Backpack

fields.label: Key features & specs
fields.placeholder: 45L, 1.2kg, waterproof ripstop, ventilated back panel, lifetime warranty.

fields.label: Target keywords
fields.placeholder: lightweight hiking backpack, waterproof daypack

template:
You are a senior e-commerce copywriter. Write a product description for "{{productName}}" that converts browsers into buyers.

## Product details
{{details}}

## Brief
- Target customer: {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.
- Length: {{length}}.
- Include: {{elements}}.

## Requirements
- Lead with the core benefit, not the feature; translate every spec into what the customer gets.
- Use sensory, specific language; avoid clichés and unverifiable superlatives.
- Address the top purchase objection naturally.
{{#if seo}}- Optimize for search: weave in {{#if keywords}}these keywords without stuffing: {{keywords}}{{else}}relevant natural keywords{{/if}}, and include an SEO title tag and meta description.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
The finished description in the requested structure{{#if seo}}, plus the title tag and meta description{{/if}}.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if a critical product detail is missing.

label: Category Page Copy
description: Craft category landing copy that ranks and guides shoppers

fields.label: Category name
fields.placeholder: Women's Running Shoes

fields.label: Assortment & positioning
fields.placeholder: 200+ SKUs across road, trail, and stability; mid-to-premium price points.

fields.label: Primary keywords
fields.placeholder: women's running shoes, best running shoes for women

template:
You are an e-commerce SEO content strategist. Write category page copy for "{{category}}".

## Assortment & positioning
{{context}}

## Brief
- Target shopper: {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.
- Produce these sections: {{sections}}.
- Long-form copy is placed {{placement}}; keep above-the-fold copy tight.
- Primary keywords: {{keywords}}.

## Requirements
- Help the shopper self-select quickly; clarify how the assortment is organized.
- Be genuinely useful (buying considerations, fit, use cases), not keyword filler.
{{#if internalLinks}}- Recommend internal links to relevant subcategories and guides with descriptive anchor text.
{{/if}}- Provide an SEO title tag and meta description.

## Deliverables
All requested sections, formatted with headings, plus the title tag and meta description.

Proceed with sensible defaults; ask only if the positioning is unclear.

label: Lifecycle Email Flow
description: Design an automated email/SMS flow with triggers and copy

fields.label: Flow goal
fields.placeholder: Convert first-time buyers into repeat customers

fields.label: Brand & product context
fields.placeholder: DTC coffee subscription; AOV $35; replenishment cycle ~30 days.

template:
You are a lifecycle marketing strategist. Design a {{flowType}} flow whose goal is: {{flowGoal}}.

## Brand context
{{brand}}

## Brief
- Audience segment: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Channels: {{channels}}.
- {{messageCount}} messages in the sequence.

## For the flow specify
1. The entry trigger and exit conditions.
2. Each message: timing/delay, channel, goal, subject line (and preview text for email), and full body copy with a single clear CTA.
3. Branching or conditional logic where it improves results.
{{#if includeOffer}}4. Where and how to introduce an incentive without training customers to wait for discounts.
{{/if}}5. The primary success metric for the flow and one A/B test worth running.

## Deliverables
The complete flow spec plus ready-to-use copy for every message.

Proceed with proven lifecycle defaults; ask only if the brand context is insufficient.

label: CRO Audit
description: Audit a page for conversion friction and prioritized fixes

fields.label: Page description & current layout
fields.placeholder: PDP with gallery, price, variant picker, ATC, then reviews; no trust badges; slow on mobile.

fields.label: Known data / problem
fields.placeholder: Mobile conversion 0.8% vs 2.1% desktop; 60% add-to-cart but high cart abandonment.

template:
You are a conversion-rate-optimization consultant. Audit this {{pageType}} for conversion friction.

## Page
{{pageDetails}}

## Context
- Primary audience: {{audience}}
- Primary device: {{device}}.
- Focus areas: {{focus}}.
{{#if metrics}}- Known data / problem: {{metrics}}
{{/if}}
## Deliver the audit as
1. The most likely conversion blockers, each with the friction it causes and the evidence or principle behind it.
2. Prioritized recommendations ranked by expected impact vs. effort (ICE-style), with a specific change for each.
3. Quick wins separated from larger structural changes.
{{#if includeTests}}4. Concrete A/B test hypotheses ("changing X will improve Y because Z") with the metric to measure.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
The full prioritized audit, specific enough to action without further clarification.

Proceed with established CRO principles; state assumptions rather than asking unless blocked.

label: Merchandising Plan
description: Plan assortment, pricing and promotions for a period

fields.label: Plan scope
fields.placeholder: Q4 holiday plan for the outerwear category

fields.label: Business context & inventory
fields.placeholder: Overstocked on last season's parkas; new fleece line landing in October; target +20% category revenue.

template:
You are an e-commerce merchandising lead. Build a merchandising plan: {{scope}}.

## Business context
{{context}}

## Brief
- Target customer: {{audience}}
- Primary objective: {{objective}}.
- Levers in scope: {{levers}}.
- Time horizon: {{horizon}}.

## The plan should cover
1. Assortment strategy: hero products, what to push, hold, or phase out, and why.
2. Pricing and promotion strategy aligned to the objective, protecting margin where it matters.
3. On-site execution: placement, navigation, and cross-sell tactics.
{{#if includeCalendar}}4. A week-by-week promo calendar across the {{horizon}}.
{{/if}}5. KPIs to track and a simple plan to react if targets are missed.

## Deliverables
The complete plan, decision-ready and tied to the stated objective.

Make confident, data-informed recommendations; ask only if the business context is insufficient.

label: Review Response
description: Draft on-brand replies to customer reviews and ratings

fields.label: The customer review
fields.placeholder: Paste the review text here, including the star rating if known.

fields.label: Product / order context
fields.placeholder: Ceramic non-stick pan, replaced under warranty 2 weeks ago

fields.label: Resolution / next step offered
fields.placeholder: Offer a replacement and a prepaid return label

template:
You are a customer-experience specialist who writes public review responses that protect the brand and win trust.

## The review
{{reviewText}}

## Context
- Sentiment: {{sentiment}}.
{{#if product}}- Product/order: {{product}}.
{{/if}}- Audience reading the reply (future shoppers too): {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.

## How to respond
- Address the reviewer by acknowledging their specific points; never use a generic template.
{{#if sentiment == "Negative"}}- Take genuine ownership without being defensive or making excuses; do not dispute their experience publicly.
{{/if}}{{#if sentiment == "Positive"}}- Reinforce what they loved and gently encourage continued engagement.
{{/if}}{{#if offerRemedy}}- Offer a concrete next step: {{#if resolution}}{{resolution}}{{else}}an appropriate, specific remedy{{/if}}.
{{/if}}{{#if moveOffline}}- Invite them to continue privately via a clear channel for anything sensitive.
{{/if}}- Keep it concise; remember future buyers are reading this.

## Deliverables
The ready-to-post reply{{#if sentiment == "Negative"}}, plus one shorter alternative version{{/if}}.

Proceed with sound CX judgment; ask only if the review is unintelligible.

label: Abandoned Cart Sequence
description: Recover lost sales with a timed, persuasive cart sequence

fields.label: Brand & product context
fields.placeholder: Premium skincare; AOV $70; common objection is price and uncertainty about results.

template:
You are a retention marketer who writes high-recovery abandoned-cart sequences.

## Brand context
{{brand}}

## Brief
- Audience segment: {{audience}}
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Channels: {{channels}}.
- {{messageCount}} messages total.
- Primary objection to overcome: {{objection}}.
- Discount strategy: {{discountStage}}.

## For each message provide
1. Send timing relative to abandonment, and channel.
2. The angle/goal of that message and how it counters the {{objection}} objection.
3. Subject line + preview text (email) or full SMS copy, plus body copy with one clear CTA back to the cart.
{{#if dynamicCart}}4. Where to insert dynamic cart contents (use {item} style placeholders).
{{/if}}
## Notes
- Escalate persuasion across the sequence; don't repeat the same message louder.
- Respect the discount strategy precisely.

## Deliverables
The full sequence spec with ready-to-use copy for every message.

Proceed with proven retention defaults; ask only if the brand context is insufficient.

label: Product Bundle Strategy
description: Design profitable product bundles with positioning and pricing

fields.label: Products & catalog context
fields.placeholder: Skincare line: cleanser $20, serum $45, moisturizer $35; serum is the hero, cleanser overstocked.

template:
You are an e-commerce merchandising strategist who designs bundles that lift profit, not just units. Design product bundles.

## Catalog context
{{catalog}}

## Brief
- Objective: {{goal}}.
- Target customer: {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.
- Produce {{bundleCount}} bundles using a {{pricingModel}} approach.

## Requirements
- Each bundle has a clear logic: why these products belong together for this customer and objective.
- Specify the contents, the bundle price vs. à la carte, the implied discount, and the customer-perceived value.
{{#if protectMargin}}- Keep blended margin healthy; flag any bundle that erodes margin and how to offset it (anchor item, attach a hero SKU).
{{/if}}- Map each bundle to where it should appear on-site (PDP add-on, dedicated page, cart upsell).
{{#if includeCopy}}- Provide a name and 2-3 lines of sell copy per bundle.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
The bundle lineup with contents, pricing, placement{{#if includeCopy}}, and copy{{/if}}, plus the single bundle to lead with and why.

Make confident, margin-aware recommendations; ask only if the catalog context is insufficient.

label: Size & Fit Guide
description: Write a clear size guide that reduces fit-related returns

fields.label: Product / category
fields.placeholder: Women's slim-fit denim jeans

fields.label: Fit details, sizing data & known issues
fields.placeholder: Runs small in waist, true to length; XS-XXL; common return reason is tight hips.

template:
You are an e-commerce content specialist focused on reducing fit-related returns. Write a size and fit guide for "{{product}}".

## Fit details & known issues
{{fitContext}}

## Brief
- Target customer: {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.
- Use {{units}} measurements.
- Include: {{elements}}.

## Requirements
- Be concrete and reassuring; the goal is the right size on the first order, not just information.
- The size chart must be unambiguous, with the body measurement each column maps to.
- Address the stated fit issues head-on (e.g. "runs small — size up if between sizes") rather than burying them.
- Plain how-to-measure steps a customer can follow with a tape measure at home.
{{#if addFitFinder}}- Add a short fit-finder: 2-3 questions that route the shopper to a recommended size.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
The complete on-page size guide in the requested structure, plus one line of microcopy for the PDP that nudges correct sizing.

Proceed with sensible defaults; ask only if the sizing data is insufficient to build the chart.

label: Marketplace Listing
description: Optimize a marketplace listing for search rank and conversion

fields.label: Product
fields.placeholder: Stainless steel insulated water bottle 32oz

fields.label: Features, specs & differentiators
fields.placeholder: Double-wall vacuum, 24h cold, leakproof lid, BPA-free; vs. competitors: lifetime warranty.

fields.label: Target keywords / search terms
fields.placeholder: insulated water bottle, leakproof flask, gym water bottle

template:
You are a marketplace listing optimization specialist. Optimize a {{marketplace}} listing for "{{productName}}".

## Features, specs & differentiators
{{details}}

## Brief
- Target customer: {{audience}}
- Brand tone: {{tone}}.
- Produce: {{sections}}.
{{#if keywords}}- Target keywords / search terms: {{keywords}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Front-load the title with the highest-intent keywords and the core differentiator; stay readable, not stuffed.
- Bullets lead with the benefit, then the proof/spec; cover the top buyer questions and objections.
- Long description builds desire and trust with scannable structure; no unverifiable claims.
{{#if compliance}}- Respect {{marketplace}} listing policies: no prohibited claims, no competitor mentions, no contact info, correct character limits.
{{/if}}- Weave keywords in naturally where shoppers and the algorithm both benefit.

## Deliverables
All requested sections, formatted and ready to paste into {{marketplace}}, plus one note on the single biggest conversion lever for this listing.

Proceed with proven marketplace best practices; ask only if a critical product detail is missing.
